title: Cactus depressus
taxonomy: Cactus depressus is classified within the family Cactaceae, which is a group of flowering plants known as cacti. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics, such as its succulent stems and spines, as well as its growth habits. Cactus depressus is a member of the genus Pediocactus, which is a group of small to medium-sized cacti native to North America. The species is further classified as Pediocactus depressus, and it is often found in arid regions with poor soil. Within the kingdom Plantae, Cactus depressus is also classified as a member of the subclass Caryophyllidae, which includes a wide range of flowering plants.

The Prickly Pear Cactus (Opuntia spp.) is a type of flowering plant in the cactus family, characterized by its flat, rounded stems and sharp spines. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of the Prickly Pear Cactus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and steroidal saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
